channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Perros y salchichas alegran una carrera de trineos en Alaska
item.description: A lo largo de cerca de 1600 kilómetros, los alaskeños sacan parrillas, sirven bebidas y animan a sus equipos favoritos en una celebración que crea comunidad y apoya a las economías locales.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Petersen and Emily Mesner

media:content.media:credit: Emily Mesner para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Los lugareños abarrotan las calles de Anchorage y otras paradas a lo largo de la ruta de la Iditarod, creando una fiesta que parece no tener fin.

item.title: La policía británica se negó a investigar a Epstein. ¿Por qué?
item.description: Las autoridades de Londres entrevistaron en tres ocasiones a Virginia Giuffre por sus acusaciones sobre Jeffrey Epstein, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor y Ghislaine Maxwell, pero nunca se abrió una investigación penal.
item.dc:creator: Lizzie Dearden

media:content.media:credit: Joe Schildhorn/Patrick McMullan, vía Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Jeffrey Epstein y Ghislaine Maxwell en Nueva York en 2005. Maxwell cumple una condena de 20 años por su papel en la operación de tráfico sexual de Epstein.

item.title: La industria del bienestar de las mascotas está en auge. También sus precios
item.description: Los dueños de mascotas están extendiendo sus creencias y prácticas de salud a sus amigos peludos.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Giang and Sophia June

media:content.media:credit: Amy Lombard for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Vivian is grooming her dog, Porcini, a 2-year-old Norwich terrier, who requires a technique called hand stripping that not many groomers can do.

item.title: At 13, He Was Selling Sneakers. At 18, He’s Facing Terror Charges.
item.description: In February, Emir Balat disappeared from the online marketplace he frequented. Weeks later, he was arrested in a homemade-bomb attack near Gracie Mansion.
item.dc:creator: Andy Newman
item.category: Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)

media:content.media:description: Emir Balat lit what the police say was a homemade bomb at a protest in Manhattan on Saturday.

item.title: Have a Complaint About Your Apartment? You Can Tell Mayor Mamdani.
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani attended the third “rental rip-off” hearing in New York City, sitting down with three tenants to hear their woes and assuaging protesters.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani heard directly from Bronx apartment dwellers about the worst of their housing issues, which included rats, insects, broken heating units and leaks.

item.title: Picture Prompt | Passing Notes
item.description: Tell us a story, real or made up, that is inspired by this image.
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Could Free Child Care Last the Full Day? Some Working Parents Hope So.
item.description: New York City will start offering free child care for 2-year-olds this fall. A question for the Mamdani administration is whether it will be available past 2:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons

media:content.media:credit: Janice Chung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Stephanie Spence, a teacher at Early Bird Educational Center in Queens, leads pre-K students through a science experiment.

item.title: He Was Convicted in a Fatal Hit and Run. Why Isn’t He in Prison?
item.description: A New Jersey jury found Harris Jacobs guilty of leaving the scene of an accident after he struck a pedestrian with his S.U.V. But jurors would not have the final word.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully and Jonah Markowitz

media:content.media:description: The scene of the crash where Orlando Fraga-Seruti was fatally struck.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 12, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: At China’s Big Political Meeting, a Rare Debate About Inequality
item.description: China’s plan to raise pensions for farmers by less than $3 a month prompted rare criticism from lawmakers about the country’s threadbare social safety net.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang

media:content.media:credit: Andrea Verdelli for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Chinese government said it would increase the minimum basic pension for rural residents by 20 yuan a month, or about $3, to a grand total of 163 yuan a month.

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘epitome’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 210 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Addresses the Notion of a Drone Strike on California
item.description: “Isn’t this how ‘Ironman 3’ started?” Kimmel said after the F.B.I. warned state officials tobb prepare for a retaliatory Iranian drone strike on the West Coast ahead of Sunday’s Academy Awards.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Word of the Day: epitome
item.description: This word has appeared in 210 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Oil Tops $100 a Barrel as Attacks Spread Across Middle East
item.description: Global oil prices surged despite pledges from the United States and other countries to release reserves. Airstrikes shook Beirut and Tehran, and more ships were attacked in the Persian Gulf.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times

item.title: Israeli Attacks Rock Beirut, Displacing Hundreds of Thousands
item.description: Explosions shook Lebanon’s capital, Beirut, from Wednesday night into Thursday morning as Israel unleashed a wave of attacks that it said targeted Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Dahiya, a densely populated residential area of southern Beirut, Lebanon, on Wednesday night.

item.title: Las acciones de Trump en Irán y Venezuela muestran los límites de las sanciones de EE. UU.
item.description: El enorme poder estadounidense. es capaz de debilitar la economía de un adversario, pero no basta para derrocar a sus líderes.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Observando las secuelas de un ataque en Teherán la semana pasada. Habiendo llegado al límite de sus herramientas de sanciones este año, el gobierno de Trump ha optado en su lugar por utilizar la fuerza militar en lugares como Irán y Venezuela.

item.title: Trump Isn’t Ready for What He’s Starting in Cuba
item.description: Catastrophe in Cuba is not a foregone conclusion.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Sabatini and Katrin Hansing

item.title: Latinoamérica ya tiene un socio importante, y no es Trump
item.description: Los esfuerzos del presidente Donald Trump por obligar al hemisferio occidental a alinearse con EE. UU. seguirán quedándose cortos.
item.dc:creator: Oliver Stuenkel

media:content.media:credit: Ilustración fotográfica de Philotheus Nisch para The New York Times; fotografía fuente de Eric Lee para The New York Times

item.title: Top-End Auction Sales Help Pull Global Art Market Out of Slump, Study Says
item.description: The top drivers included a $2.2 billion auction week in New York and strong fall fairs, according to the annual Art Basel and UBS report.
item.dc:creator: Scott Reyburn

media:content.media:credit: Dmitry Kostyukov for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Art Basel Paris fair at the Grand Palais last October.

item.title: A Third of Americans Have Cut Spending or Borrowed Money for Health Care
item.description: As medical costs rise, more than 80 million people have made sacrifices like skipping meals and driving less, a new survey finds.
item.dc:creator: Reed Abelson

media:content.media:description: A recent poll found that health care costs topped a list of the public’s economic anxieties, above concerns about the prices of food and groceries, gas and utilities.

item.title: How to Afford Housing in London: Multiple Roommates, No Living Room
item.description: High prices have pushed more Londoners to live with roommates well into their late 20s and 30s — if they can afford to move out of their parents’ homes at all.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Schmidt

media:content.media:credit: Joseph Horton for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mr. Darragh pays 900 pounds, or about $1,225, a month for his share of a six-bedroom house in a northwest London neighborhood.

item.title: Corrections: March 12, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Thursday, March 12, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Ditching the Brooms And Trusting Your Aim
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, March 12, 2026.

item.title: Oil Keeps Pushing Higher Despite Global Release of Reserves
item.description: A worldwide pledge by governments to tap emergency reserves on Wednesday has not stemmed oil’s rise.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:description: Gas prices over $5 a gallon in Los Angeles, California, on Wednesday. Gas prices rose on Wednesday to a national average of $3.58 a gallon, according to the AAA motor club.

item.title: Washington State Passes ‘Millionaires’ Tax’
item.description: It would be the first income tax in Washington, affecting an estimated 20,000 households. Some of the wealthiest are leaving for Florida.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin

media:content.media:description: Howard Schultz, the former Starbucks chief executive, said on social media that he was moving to Florida.

item.title: Haitian President’s Widow Recalls Killers Spoke Spanish and Used Nicknames
item.description: The prosecution used testimony by Martine Moïse about the language she heard on the night her husband was killed to support its claim that the assassination was carried out by a hired Colombian hit squad.
item.dc:creator: David C. Adams

media:content.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Martine Moïse, left, and her son in Port-au-Prince, Haiti, in 2021, after the attack that killed her husband.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 12, 2026
item.description: Joe Marquez comes down to earth.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:description: Are you looking at today’s crossword through rose-, uh, daisy-colored glasses?

item.title: En Canadá, dos personas mueren después de una donación pagada de plasma
item.description: Los pacientes murieron tras donar plasma en clínicas gestionadas por Grifols, empresa española de salud que tiene 17 centros en Canadá.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai and Roni Caryn Rabin

media:content.media:description: Las clínicas de plasma estaban gestionadas por Grifols, una empresa de salud española.

item.title: Trump’s Tour of States Is About More Than the Midterms
item.description: Beyond talking about the economy and voters’ hardships, the president is showing that he still has control over the Republican Party.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater

media:content.media:description: President Trump at Verst Logistics, a packaging facility in Hebron, Ky., on Wednesday.

item.title: René Redzepi Steps Down at Noma Amid Allegations of Past Abuse
item.description: After 23 years running the Copenhagen restaurant, widely considered one of the most innovative and important in the world, the chef is leaving.
item.dc:creator: Julia Moskin

media:content.media:description: Mr. Redzepi led Noma to countless accolades, including being named No. 1 restaurant in the world five times by World’s 50 Best.

item.title: Israel Bombards Lebanese Capital in Latest Round of Strikes
item.description: Lebanon’s health ministry said at least seven people were killed in the Beirut attacks early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack and Dayana Iwaza
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Dahiya, a densely populated residential area of southern Beirut, Lebanon, on Wednesday night.

item.title: U.S. to Release 172 Million Barrels of Oil From Strategic Reserves, Energy Dept. Says
item.description: The move aims to prevent prices from rising further because of the war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: People filling up at a gas station in Los Angeles on Wednesday.

item.title: Pentagon Bars Photographers From Briefings on Iran War
item.description: The decision is the latest move by the Pentagon to restrict access inside the department for journalists.
item.dc:creator: Erik Wemple
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth walking into the East Room of the White House last week. Mr. Hegseth has taken an adversarial stance toward the news media since his first days of running the Pentagon.

item.title: Preliminary Inquiry Finds U.S. Responsible for Iran School Attack
item.description: An ongoing report determined that the United States was responsible for a Tomahawk missile strike that killed at least 175, most of them children, according to the preliminary findings of a Pentagon investigation.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Fall/Winter Highlights From Paris
item.description: Our chief fashion critic, Vanessa Friedman, reflects on her nine days at Paris Fashion Week.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman, Gabriel Blanco and Laura Salaberry

item.title: U.N. Security Council Condemns Iran’s Retaliatory Strikes in the Middle East
item.description: In an overwhelming vote, the council backed a resolution condemning Iran. A Russian proposal calling for an end to the war that didn’t assign blame or even name the parties, was rejected.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The United Nations Security Council during a meeting on Wednesday.

item.title: Noma Faces Los Angeles Protest Over Allegations of Past Abuse by Its Founder
item.description: At the vaunted restaurant’s pop-up in the Silver Lake neighborhood, fallout from recent reports continued.
item.dc:creator: Meghan McCarron and Julia Moskin

media:content.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: At the protest he helped organize, Jason Ignacio White, center, read from an open letter to Noma.

item.title: Looting Lululemon: Thieves Target Athleisure Stores in New York City
item.description: Thousands of dollars’ worth of merchandise was stolen in a series of recent thefts at Lululemon and Alo stores in Manhattan and Brooklyn, the police said.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Maag

media:content.media:description: There were four thefts in 15 days at the Lululemon clothing store in the Williamsburg neighborhood of Brooklyn, the police said.

item.title: Countries Scramble to Shore Up Global Oil Supplies as Ships Are Attacked
item.description: The United States and Israel launched more strikes against Iran, where crowds mourned military commanders killed in the war. Israel also bombed targets in Lebanon, where the death toll climbed.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott, Abdi Latif Dahir, Eric Schmitt and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A navy ship from the United Arab Emirates sailed next to a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday.

item.title: California Officials See No Imminent Threat of Drone Attacks by Iran
item.description: Just before the war began, the Federal Bureau of Investigation told state officials that Iran might respond with drone attacks on California. No specific, credible threats have been identified.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett, Laurel Rosenhall, Soumya Karlamangla and Shawn Hubler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Gov. Gavin Newsom of California speaking at a news conference in Hayward, Calif., last week.

item.title: Two People Die After Paid Plasma Donation at Clinics in Canada
item.description: Grifols, a Spanish health care company, operates clinics in Canada that collect blood plasma from donors in exchange for an honorarium.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai and Roni Caryn Rabin

media:content.media:description: The plasma clinics were operated by Grifols, a Spanish health care company.

item.title: Latinos Reshaped Sleepy Hollow. Now They May Reshape the Town Board.
item.description: The Town of Mount Pleasant has settled a lawsuit under New York’s new Voting Rights Act, agreeing to change how its town board is elected.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford

media:content.media:credit: Gregg Vigliotti for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Silvana Tapia, left, and Sergio Serratto, right, were among the plaintiffs who sued the Town of Mount Pleasant.

item.title: What Happened in the U.S.-Israeli War on Iran on Wednesday
item.description: Israel and the Iran-backed armed group Hezbollah in Lebanon traded strikes on Wednesday. Three ships were hit near the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil route.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The aftermath of an Israeli airstrike on Wednesday in Ruwais, a neighborhood in the southern suburbs of Beirut, Lebanon.

item.title: South Carolina’s 2028 Primary Is Already on Some Democrats’ Minds
item.description: The state’s 2028 primary is likely to be important, and is already on the minds of some Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

media:content.media:credit: Travis Dove for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: South Carolina is a favorite to vote early in Democrats’ 2028 presidential primary race.

item.title: Strong or Weak? How Trump Picks His Battles.
item.description: Despite his tough talk, President Trump has consistently made allowances for countries he sees as powerful or dominant.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: President Trump with President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia during a meeting in Alaska last summer. Mr. Trump’s deference to Russia highlights a pattern that has crystallized in recent months as he launches military action overseas.

item.title: Ex-Officer Who Took Nude Images From Phones in Traffic Stops Is Sentenced
item.description: The former Missouri police officer, Julian Alcala, was sentenced to two years in prison and now faces civil lawsuits from several of the 20 victims the authorities identified.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk

media:content.media:credit: Bryan Birks for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Thomas F. Eagleton U.S. courthouse in St. Louis, where a former Missouri police officer, Julian Alcala, was sentenced on Wednesday.

item.title: Initial Military Investigation Faults the U.S. For a School Strike in Iran
item.description: Also, world leaders agree to tap oil reserves. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Health care workers holding photos of schoolgirls killed by the strike on their school in Minab, Iran.

item.title: How Trump Is Using the Paxton-Cornyn Race to Squeeze the Senate Over the SAVE Act
item.description: The president has yet to make an endorsement in the contest between John Cornyn and Ken Paxton as he tries to push the Senate to pass a bill requiring voters to show identification at the polls.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher

media:content.media:credit: New York Times photographs by Tamir Kalifa and Al Drago
media:content.media:description: President Trump is leveraging his potential endorsement in the Republican primary race for Senate between Ken Paxton, the Texas attorney general, and Senator John Cornyn to ratchet up the pressure on Senate Republicans to pass a bill that would impose strict voter ID requirements.

item.title: At Least Three Ships Struck Around Strait of Hormuz
item.description: At least three ships were hit in and around the Persian Gulf on Wednesday, according to a British maritime monitoring group. Iran appeared to claim responsibility for the attack on the Mayuree Naree.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Royal Thai Navy, via Associated Press

item.title: Iran War Maps: Tracking the Mideast Conflict
item.description: Maps show where U.S. and Israel have struck Iran, and where Iran has retaliated.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
